pi_items.source: Verified by reading the export_result.sh script which writes the final output to this file path.
Task description identifies the user persona as an Infection Prevention Analyst managing disease outbreaks, aligning with Epidemiologists and Clinical Data Managers.
Task operates within a hospital system context using EHR (Electronic Health Record) schemas.

Verified via manual calculation of synthetic patient time-series logic inside setup_task.sh. Subjects 200, 202, and 400 have >12 hours of temporal overlap with infected patients in the exact same ward.

Verified via manual calculation of synthetic patient time-series logic inside setup_task.sh. Out of the 3 exposed patients, exactly 2 (subjects 200 and 400) are currently admitted (dischtime IS NULL) and require isolation orders.

Task description explicitly requires creating a Materialized View (WARD_HOTSPOT_MV), a PL/SQL procedure (PROC_FLAG_ISOLATION), an Identity Column for ISOLATION_ORDERS.order_id, and temporal mathematical logic for calculating patient overlap hours.

privileged_info_summary: The data for this task is synthetically generated via a shell script using PL/SQL inserts into an Oracle database. The data models a hospital EHR schema including ADMISSIONS, TRANSFERS, and MICROBIOLOGY tables with 2 index infection cases (MRSA and C.DIFF). Manual analysis of the synthetic temporal data confirms exactly 3 patients (subject_ids 200, 202, 400) meet the >12-hour exposure duration criteria in the same ward as an infected patient. Cross-referencing these exposed patients with current admission statuses confirms that exactly 2 patients (subject_ids 200, 400) have an active admission (dischtime IS NULL) and require isolation orders. The requested Oracle features—Temporal Logic, Materialized Views, PL/SQL, and Identity Columns—are explicitly defined technical requirements in the task description.
